commands.command: python -m unittest tests.test_maintenance_lanes tests.test_org_sources tests.test_software_update tests.test_org_automation
commands.summary: 26 focused tests passed.

findings: Failed update state can be retried automatically by Architect without a fresh explicit user prepare action if update_available and user_requested remain true.
Maintenance lane runners release locks on normal return paths but do not wrap the whole stateful run in try/finally, so unexpected exceptions rely on stale-lock recovery.
counterexamples: architect_update_check -> update_failed -> architect_update_check can apply again from failed state when update_available=true and user_requested=true.
skipped_steps: formal flowguard package run skipped: Python module flowguard is not installed in this workspace
friction_points: The fallback model is executable but not a formal flowguard package run.
next_actions: Decide whether failed updates should require a fresh user prepare action before retry.
Consider wrapping stateful maintenance lane bodies in finally release/status handling.

commands.summary: 44 focused tests passed.
218 tests passed.
Desktop KB check passed with 138 entries.
Install health checklist passed.
No whitespace errors; PowerShell reported expected CRLF normalization warnings for touched files.

findings: Implemented failed update as a terminal waiting state for the same target by clearing user_requested and preventing Architect from retrying until the user prepares again.
A newly discovered remote target after a failed update now moves back to available and still requires fresh user preparation before upgrading.
Dream, Architect, organization contribution, and organization maintenance now write failed lane status and release their lane lock from a finally path on unexpected exceptions.
counterexamples: Resolved previous failed-update auto-retry trace: failed same target now emits update_failed_awaiting_user instead of upgrading.
skipped_steps: formal flowguard package run skipped: Python module flowguard is not installed in this workspace; user requested not to address that tooling gap now
friction_points: A first mechanical attempt to wrap large maintenance functions matched the first return instead of the full function boundary; py_compile caught it, and the implementation was redone using function-boundary-aware wrapping.
next_actions: Keep the formal flowguard package/toolchain gap as a separate follow-up owned outside this fix.
Use function-boundary-aware edits for large stateful functions when adding outer try/finally guards.

findings: The current system has legitimate non-Sleep card creation surfaces: manual candidate capture, Dream candidate creation, and organization adoption.
Sleep candidate creation plus an applied i18n cleanup closes the zh-CN display gap in the model.
A Sleep pass that creates or sees cards but finalizes without an applied i18n plan can leave English-only cards visible in the UI until a later i18n pass.
counterexamples: card_created_by_manual -> not_sleep_i18n_owner -> active_task_done violates the strict all-cards-created-by-Sleep hypothesis.
card_created_by_sleep -> sleep_i18n_cleanup_skipped -> sleep_finalized_with_missing_i18n violates the Sleep-finalization-without-missing-i18n invariant.
skipped_steps: production conformance replay skipped: this task was read-only diagnosis and did not change production card/i18n code.
friction_points: ScenarioRun summaries expose ok as absent/null, so the model uses compact label summaries for exact scenario traces.
next_actions: Treat missing zh-CN on current cards as a Sleep i18n follow-up gap unless a direct card creation path intentionally wrote the card after the last cleanup.
Consider making Sleep finalization or reporting fail loudly when review-i18n actions remain after candidate creation or semantic text changes.

counterexamples: unreviewed candidate backlog
promotion without semantic review
unreviewed or dropped Dream handoff
weak Dream promotion
Architect ready-for-patch without outlet
route drift before card creation or finalization
release readiness with install drift, unexpected org pause, or stale lane state
findings: candidate_backlog_pressure: 183 candidates versus 2 public and 1 private cards.
sleep_review_pressure: latest Sleep run kb-sleep-20260507T100105Z produced 1446 candidate actions and 480 apply-eligible actions.
dream_sleep_handoff_open: latest Dream run kb-dream-20260507T110225Z has 4 handoffs, 3 review-ready handoffs, and 3 strong/moderate handoffs.
architect_execution_outlet_gap: 12 ready-for-patch proposals exist, with 0 ready-for-apply and 0 sandbox-ready packets.
route_drift_pressure: 478 blank-route history events, 12 root-direct cards, 5 dotted card routes, and undeclared route families.
install_policy_metadata_drift: maintenance rollup install report has 19 issues.
stale_running_lane_without_lock: kb-org-contribute lane status says running without the corresponding lock.
Allowed note: user-paused organization automations kb-org-contribute and kb-org-maintenance are not treated as failures by themselves.
friction_points: FlowGuard invariants are evaluated over intermediate states, so governance-debt invariants needed an explicit terminal state through finalize_governance or mark_release_ready.
next_actions: Use the new governance model as the preflight gate before changing Khaos Brain maintenance mechanics.
Address model findings through explicit future Khaos Brain changes rather than treating live projection failure as a model failure.

Existing older FlowGuard models were not refactored; the new model was added as an isolated governance projection.

findings: Mistake, weak-path, missed-instruction, failed-validation, tool/skill-misuse, user-correction, and correction episode evidence is now explicit highest-priority KB postflight evidence.
Successful reusable observations remain allowed and are not suppressed by mistake-first priority.
next_actions: Keep the mistake-priority install checklist as part of strong_session_defaults for future machine setup.
task_summary: Prioritize Codex mistakes and corrections in predictive KB postflight prompts
trigger_reason: The KB postflight workflow changes prompt and installer behavior; FlowGuard modeled the decision rule so mistake/correction evidence outranks success evidence while success observations remain allowed.

commands.summary: flowguard schema version 1.0 is importable.
Accepted Sleep generalization sequences passed; same-project, project-local, skill-specific, old-card, and missing-scope bad variants were rejected.
Model and touched Python modules compiled.
31 focused tests passed.

counterexamples: same-project evidence treated as cross-project evidence
project-local evidence rewritten as a general rule
skill-specific evidence rewritten as a capability-independent rule
old project-shaped reusable card left without a rewrite-as-general-rule review
semantic apply allowed without scope assessment
findings: Sleep now distinguishes project-local, skill-specific, single-project-generalizable, cross-project-general, and insufficient-evidence outcomes.
Same-project repetition is chronology evidence, not automatic cross-project proof.
Skill-specific evidence remains a valid bounded rule when future invocation depends on a named Skill, plugin, connector, or tool.
Semantic review apply is blocked when card-surface decisions lack scope assessment.
next_actions: Run broader regression and install sync before release.
skipped_steps: Full regression and install sync are tracked as later release-gate steps.
task_summary: Add scoped generalization review to Sleep maintenance.
trigger_reason: Sleep maintenance changes card-candidate and semantic-review decision behavior, including same-project chronology, cross-project evidence, project-local boundaries, and skill-specific boundaries.
